gaps: No test case validates the post-DFU UI state where the 'Update' button remains visible with the same build number after a successful Charge Case firmware update on Voyager Legend 50.

No test case covers tapping the 'Update' button a second time after a successful DFU to verify the app handles the 'already up-to-date' state gracefully (e.g., showing a proper message, disabling the button, or preventing a failed re-attempt).
No test case validates that the 'Update' button is properly disabled, hidden, or its state refreshed after a successful DFU without requiring an app restart.

No test case covers the lifecycle dimension: the bug requires an app restart to clear the stale button state — no existing test validates that the button state persists incorrectly across the DFU completion lifecycle event.

No test case covers the Voyager Legend 50 device specifically for post-DFU button state behavior — existing DFU-related tests focus on Voyager Free 60+ and language-change scenarios, not the basic 'same build number re-tap' failure on Legend 50.
No test case validates the Software tab UI state transition from 'Update available' to 'Up-to-date' after a DFU completes successfully, which is the core failure point of this bug.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-LEG50-001: Verify 'Update' button is disabled/hidden after successful Charge Case DFU on Voyager Legend 50
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Legend 50 device tile to enter Device screen.
Navigate to Settings → Software.
Verify the Software tab shows 'Update available' and an 'Update' button with the current Charge Case firmware build number.
Tap 'Update' to begin the Charge Case firmware update.
Wait for the DFU to complete successfully (progress reaches 100% and success confirmation appears).
Observe the 'Update' button state on the Software tab immediately after DFU completion.
Do NOT restart the app or disconnect Bluetooth.
Verify the 'Update' button is now disabled (greyed out) or hidden.
Verify the Software tab now displays 'Up-to-date' or equivalent status text.
Verify the build number shown matches the newly installed firmware version.
generated_testcases.expected: After successful Charge Case DFU on Voyager Legend 50, the 'Update' button is disabled/hidden and the Software tab shows 'Up-to-date' status without requiring an app restart.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-LEG50-002: Verify tapping 'Update' button again after successful DFU shows graceful 'already up-to-date' message
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Legend 50 device tile to enter Device screen.
Navigate to Settings → Software.
Tap 'Update' to perform the Charge Case firmware update.
Wait for DFU to complete successfully.
Immediately tap the 'Update' button again (if still visible/enabled).
Observe the app response.
generated_testcases.expected: The app handles the re-tap gracefully: either the button is disabled so no action occurs, or tapping it shows a clear message like 'Firmware is already up-to-date' or 'No update available'. No crash, no failed update attempt, no error state requiring app restart.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-LEG50-003: Verify Software tab UI state transitions from 'Update available' to 'Up-to-date' after successful DFU on Voyager Legend 50
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Legend 50 device tile.
Navigate to Settings → Software.
Capture the initial UI state: note the presence of 'Update available' text, the 'Update' button, and the current build number.
Initiate the Charge Case firmware update by tapping 'Update'.
Wait for the DFU to reach 100% and show success confirmation.
Immediately after success, observe the Software tab UI.

Check for the following elements: status text (should now say 'Up-to-date' or 'Firmware is up to date'), 'Update' button state (should be disabled or absent), build number (should reflect the new version).
Navigate away from the Software tab (e.g., tap Audio Settings), then navigate back to Settings → Software.
Verify the 'Up-to-date' status persists after navigation.
generated_testcases.expected: The Software tab UI transitions cleanly from 'Update available' to 'Up-to-date' immediately after DFU completion. The stale 'Update' button with the old build number is not present. The state persists after navigating away and back.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-LEG50-004: Verify post-DFU button state persists incorrectly across app lifecycle without restart (bug reproduction)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Legend 50 device tile.
Navigate to Settings → Software.
Note the current Charge Case build number and the presence of the 'Update' button.
Tap 'Update' and complete the Charge Case firmware DFU successfully.
After success confirmation, observe the 'Update' button state.
If the button is still visible and enabled (bug state), tap it again.
Observe the result (expected bug: immediate failure or error).
Without restarting the app, background the app (press Home button).
Reopen the app from the recent apps list.
Navigate back to Settings → Software on the Voyager Legend 50 device.
Observe the 'Update' button state again.
Now force-kill the app (swipe from recent apps).
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile, reconnect to Voyager Legend 50.
Navigate to Settings → Software.
Observe the final 'Update' button state.
generated_testcases.expected: This test reproduces the bug: after DFU, the 'Update' button remains visible with the same build number. Tapping it causes immediate failure. Backgrounding and reopening the app does NOT fix the stale state. Only a full app restart (force-kill and relaunch) clears the stale button state and shows 'Up-to-date'.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-LEG50-005: Verify post-DFU button state across Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect on Voyager Legend 50
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Connect to Voyager Legend 50.
Navigate to Settings → Software.
Perform a successful Charge Case firmware update via DFU.
After DFU completion, do NOT restart the app.
Disconnect Bluetooth from the phone's Settings or toggle Bluetooth off.
Wait 5 seconds.
Re-enable Bluetooth and reconnect to Voyager Legend 50.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app (if not already open).
Navigate to Settings → Software.
Observe the 'Update' button state and status text.
generated_testcases.expected: After Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect, the Software tab should show 'Up-to-date' status and the 'Update' button should be disabled/hidden. The stale button state should not persist across a Bluetooth reconnection cycle.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-LEG50-006: Verify DFU button state when Charge Case is already up-to-date on initial connection (Voyager Legend 50)
steps: Ensure Voyager Legend 50 Charge Case has the latest firmware already installed.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Legend 50 device tile.
Navigate to Settings → Software.
Observe the Software tab UI.
generated_testcases.expected: When the Charge Case is already up-to-date on initial connection, the Software tab should show 'Up-to-date' status and no 'Update' button should be available. This validates the correct baseline state before any DFU is performed.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-LEG50-007: Verify tapping 'Update' button after successful DFU on a different device (Voyager Free 60+) does NOT exhibit the same bug
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Connect to a Voyager Free 60+ device.
Navigate to Settings → Software.
Perform a successful Charge Case firmware update via DFU.
After DFU completion, observe the 'Update' button state.
If the button is still visible, tap it again.
Observe the app response.
Force-kill and restart the app, reconnect to Voyager Free 60+.
Navigate to Settings → Software and confirm the correct 'Up-to-date' state.
generated_testcases.expected: This test validates whether the bug is specific to Voyager Legend 50 or affects Voyager Free 60+ as well. The expected behavior on Free 60+ may differ — document the actual result for comparison.
